assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'This is NOT a rolling upgrade — TPU multislice environments are recreated atomically' and 'All 4 slices (1,024 TPU chips total) will restart simultaneously'

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'A maintenance event on ANY slice triggers restart of ALL slices in your multislice environment' and 'Even if only 1 of your 4 slices needs maintenance, all 1,024 chips restart together'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Your training job will be completely interrupted and must restart from the last checkpoint' and describes complete environment restart

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response states 'If insufficient TPU v5e resources exist during recreation, your slices will block until hardware becomes available' and 'Downtime could extend from minutes to hours depending on TPU availability'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends 'Force a checkpoint save immediately before the maintenance window' in both preparation steps and timeline sections

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response includes specific kubectl commands for restart policies and states 'Ensure your job can automatically resume from checkpoint' with configuration examples

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response provides specific gcloud commands for maintenance exclusions and states 'Use this time to reach a natural checkpoint/stopping point in your training'

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: While the response mentions maintenance windows in the timeline section, it does not explain how to configure or control when the maintenance window occurs, nor does it suggest scheduling it during planned training breaks
